title: Amber and sandalwood soy candle
subtitle: A warm, resinous container soy candle with amber and sandalwood fragrance
excerpt: Amber and sandalwood together produce a warm, woody fragrance with a resinous depth that suits evening use. Both are base notes with good longevity in soy wax. Poured in a dark or frosted glass container, this candle has a good cold and hot throw. Standard soy container wax, fragrance at 8%.
sourceNotes: Amber and sandalwood fragrance oil in soy container wax: Bramble Berry and Candlescience formulation guides (2022, 2023). Both amber and sandalwood are predominantly base-note fragrances with good retention in soy wax and a well-documented safe profile in burn applications within IFRA category 12 limits.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Pour multiple jars from the same batch for consistent results.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Allow 48 hours before the first burn. Base-note fragrances like amber and sandalwood benefit from the longer cure time.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: The candle keeps several years. Amber and sandalwood are robust base notes and hold their fragrance well. Keep the lid on between burns. Store away from heat and direct light.

glossaryTerms.definition: The first burn sets the pool pattern for all subsequent burns. Burn until the wax pool reaches the full jar diameter to prevent tunnelling.
The temperature at which wax is poured. Soy container wax pours cleanly at 60 to 65 degrees C with good adhesion to the glass.

content.text: Amber and sandalwood are both base-note fragrances, which means they have a long lifespan in the wax and throw well in a room without burning off quickly. Amber contributes a warm, slightly sweet resinous quality; sandalwood adds a creamy woody note beneath it. This candle suits an evening room rather than a kitchen. Standard soy container wax process throughout.

items.prepNote: 8% of wax weight; candle-rated fragrance oil
ECO-4 or CD-12 for a 6 to 7 cm jar

content.text: Melt wax over a water bath only. Never leave melting wax unattended. Keep a kitchen fire blanket within reach.
Melt the soy wax over a water bath to 75 degrees C. Cool to 60 to 65 degrees C. Add the fragrance oil and stir for 2 minutes.
Centre the pre-tabbed wick with a wick bar. Pour at the
of 60 to 65 degrees C. Leave undisturbed for 8 hours. Trim the wick to 5 mm before the first burn.
rule: burn until the wax pool reaches the full jar diameter, around 2 to 3 hours for a 6 cm jar.
Replace the lid after each burn. Store away from heat and light. The candle keeps several years; the amber and sandalwood hold their fragrance well compared to lighter top-note fragrances.
